scope: Changes from increment 0626: vskill (appstore SKILL.md 27 fixes, src/commands/add.ts, src/eval/verdict.ts, src/installer/migrate.ts, migrate.test.ts, platform-security.ts) and vskill-platform (provider-registry.ts, crawl-worker/sources/vendor-org-discovery.js)

findings.title: Marketplace detection — triple empty catches with zero logging
findings.description: detectMarketplaceRepo() has 3 sequential attempts (Contents API, retry after 1s, raw fallback). All have empty catch {} blocks. On DNS issues, corporate proxy, or GitHub outage, all three silently fail and the function returns { isMarketplace: false }. Skills install via the wrong path with no user indication.
findings.recommendation: Add console.warn on the final (3rd) catch with the error message.

findings.title: Search API failure silently redirects to legacy registry path
findings.description: When searchSkills() fails in resolveViaSearch(), the error is caught and user redirected to installFromRegistry() with only a yellow tip. Original error (network, API 500, auth) is never logged. User may get 'skill not found' from registry when the real problem was a transient API outage.
findings.recommendation: Log console.warn with original error before fallback: `console.warn('[search] API failed, falling back to registry:', err.message)`

findings.title: Search recommendation failure swallowed — user sees bare 'not found'
findings.description: When installFromRegistry() tries 'Did you mean...' suggestions via searchSkills(), failure is caught with empty block. User sees bare 'not found' with no suggestions and no indication the suggestion system failed.
findings.recommendation: Add console.warn or note in output that suggestions are unavailable.

findings.title: 404 fallback repeats on every pagination page for personal accounts
findings.description: listOrgRepos() tries /orgs/{org}/repos on EVERY loop page. For personal accounts (like anton-abyzov), gets 404 on every page, logs 'not an org, trying users endpoint' repeatedly, then falls back to /users/{org}/repos. The fallback state is not remembered across pages. With anton-abyzov in VENDOR_ORGS, this fires on every crawl run — 300 repos = 3 extra API calls + 3 duplicate log lines wasted.
findings.recommendation: Track fallback with `let useUsersEndpoint = false;` flag. After first 404, set flag and skip org endpoint on subsequent pages.

findings.title: InstallSafetyResult missing discriminated union
findings.description: Plain intersection type allows safe:false with no reason or safe:true with a reason. A discriminated union `{ safe: true } | { safe: false; reason: string }` makes callers handle both branches exhaustively and prevents misleading null-reason failure states.
findings.recommendation: Refactor InstallSafetyResult to a discriminated union.

findings.title: Unvalidated res.json() type casts
findings.description: Multiple `const data = await res.json() as SomeType` casts with no runtime validation. API error bodies or HTML 5xx pages produce runtime errors or silently wrong data.
findings.recommendation: Add runtime shape validation (zod or type guard) before casting res.json() results.

findings.title: validateEnum uses unsafe `as T` cast
findings.description: validateEnum(value, enumObj) returns `value as T` without verifying value is actually in the enum. Non-matching values silently cast to T. NOTE: reviewer-logic found this is actually gated by allowed.has() check — may be a false positive. Needs manual verification.
findings.recommendation: Verify if allowed.has() check is present. If not, add Object.values(enumObj).includes(value) check.

findings.title: anton-abyzov placed under 'Trusted orgs' comment — but is vendor tier
findings.description: anton-abyzov has trustLevel: 'vendor' (auto-CERTIFIED, bypasses scanning) but sits under the '// Trusted orgs — exempt from taint, still scanned' comment. Future maintainers will assume scanning still runs when it does not. Directly contradicts the actual trust level.
findings.recommendation: Move anton-abyzov entry under the 'Vendor orgs' comment section.

findings.title: Skill name path traversal — validateSkillName() not called
findings.description: add command accepts skill paths like owner/repo/skillname and uses the skill name in filesystem path construction. validateSkillName() exists but is not called before the path join. Crafted name like ../../.bashrc could traverse outside the target directory.
findings.recommendation: Call validateSkillName() on the parsed skill name component before any path operations.

findings.title: SSRF via unvalidated download_url from GitHub API
findings.description: GitHub API response download_url is fetched directly without URL validation. A malicious/compromised response could point to an internal service (metadata endpoint, Redis). Should validate URL is on github.com / raw.githubusercontent.com.
findings.recommendation: Add URL allowlist: only fetch URLs matching ^https://raw\.githubusercontent\.com/ or ^https://api\.github\.com/

findings.title: Users endpoint fallback missing sort=updated
findings.description: Org endpoint URL includes sort=updated but users endpoint fallback omits it. Users repos default to sort by full_name (alphabetical). If scanning is interrupted at rate limit, most recently maintained repos may not have been scanned.
findings.recommendation: Add &sort=updated to users endpoint URL for consistency.

findings.title: Skill uninstall cleanup silently ignores removal failures
findings.description: During plugin uninstall, rmSync failures are caught silently. Skill appears as 'uninstalled' in summary but files may still exist on disk (EACCES, EBUSY). Stale skills continue to load.
findings.recommendation: Log warning and decrement removedCount on catch.

findings.title: Skill submission errors partially swallowed
findings.description: submitSkill() failures during unregistered plugin verification silently catch and show generic 'Could not submit automatically' without the original error. Auth failures or API changes go undiagnosed.
findings.recommendation: Log: console.warn(`Submission failed for ${skill.name}: ${err.message}`)

findings.title: Discovery loop outer catch completely swallowed
findings.description: Outer try/catch around entire unregistered plugin verification loop silently swallows all errors including TypeErrors that indicate bugs. Verification silently skips remaining plugins.
findings.recommendation: Log: console.warn(`Discovery error for ${unreg.name}: ${err.message}`)

findings.title: scanRepoTree conflates 'no skills' with 'API error'
findings.description: scanRepoTree returns [] both when a repo has no SKILL.md files AND when API call fails. Caller can't distinguish 'scanned successfully' from 'couldn't scan at all'. Stats show repos as 'scanned with 0 skills' when not scanned.
findings.recommendation: Return discriminated result { skills: [], error?: string } or throw on API errors so caller can track skippedCount separately.

findings.title: migrate.ts filesystem traversal — 9 empty catch blocks
findings.description: All readdirSync and lstatSync calls have empty catch blocks. Permission errors (EACCES), filesystem corruption, or I/O errors are completely silent. Migration reports success (0 errors) but may have silently skipped entire subdirectories.
findings.recommendation: Push readdirSync failures to result.errors. For lstatSync, add debug-level log.

findings.title: Duplicate .md file cleanup silently fails
findings.description: After winner .md file renamed to SKILL.md, remaining candidate .md files deletion failures are silently caught. Stale duplicate files persist alongside SKILL.md.
findings.recommendation: Push to result.errors: `result.errors.push(...)`

findings.title: String() cast on status loses type safety
findings.description: String(p.status || 'PENDING') as ProviderResult['status'] silently succeeds for any input. An unexpected status value is silently cast to an invalid union member.
findings.recommendation: Validate against known union members before casting.

findings.title: Verdict accumulation may lose partial results on error
findings.description: If an evaluator throws mid-accumulation, all accumulated verdicts are lost and caller gets unhandled rejection. For evaluation pipelines, partial results with error metadata is more useful.
findings.recommendation: Wrap individual evaluator calls in try/catch and accumulate error results.

findings.title: Users endpoint fallback doesn't validate response shape
findings.description: GitHub's user repos endpoint may return different fields than org endpoint, causing silent null references downstream.
findings.recommendation: Add shape validation or normalize both response shapes to a common interface.

findings.title: GitHub token may be logged in debug error conditions
findings.recommendation: Sanitize Authorization header from error objects before logging.

findings.title: No timeout on skill download fetch
findings.recommendation: Add AbortController with 30s timeout.

findings.title: 'must be kept in sync' comment contradicts intentional divergence
findings.recommendation: Update comment: 'Based on provider-registry.ts VENDOR_ORG_IDS, minus large orgs excluded below.'

findings.title: 5 telemetry .catch(() => {}) calls with zero diagnostic logging
findings.recommendation: Add debug-mode logging: .catch((err) => { if (process.env.VSKILL_DEBUG) console.warn('[telemetry]', err.message); })

findings.title: Git remote URL detection silently falls through
findings.recommendation: Acceptable as-is. INFO-level.

findings.title: Agent companion file fetch failures silently skipped
findings.recommendation: Add debug-level warn for failed fetches.

findings.title: 6 GitHub API probe failures silently swallowed in plugin discovery
findings.recommendation: For lines 1524/1536, add combined warning when both fail.

findings.title: Test fixtures use `as any` casts
findings.recommendation: Use typed fixture builders or Partial<T> with required fields.

findings.title: overallVerdict cast allows invalid enum values (same pattern as M-011)
findings.recommendation: Validate before cast.

findings.title: Error type narrowing uses `as Error` instead of instanceof guard
findings.recommendation: Use `if (e instanceof Error)` guard before accessing error properties.

findings.title: All 26 ACs pass — increment 0626 fully compliant
findings.description: 26/26 acceptance criteria verified. Both SKILL.md copies byte-identical. No scope creep.

findings.title: Vendor tier bypass is intentional — needs documentation comment
findings.recommendation: Add comment: '// Owner org — vendor tier intentionally bypasses scanning for self-published skills'

findings.title: platform-security.ts returns null correctly with logging (by design)
verdict.ts — pure computation, no silent failures possible
migrate.test.ts — comprehensive 28-test coverage. Missing: permission-denied scenario tests.
asc screenshots delete lacks --confirm for consistency
findings.recommendation: Add --confirm flag to match other destructive operations.

findings.title: GitHub Actions YAML uses unquoted --app ${{ vars.APP_ID }}
findings.recommendation: Quote: --app "${{ vars.APP_ID }}"
